LAW AND MOTION, 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE KNOWS GORDON ATKINSON. THEIR CHILDREN WENT TO SCHOOL TOGETHER AND THEY SERVED ON THE SAME NON-PROFIT BOARD. THESE FACTS DO NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFED. DEFENDANT GCA SAVVIAN ADVISORS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITH 10 LEAVE TO ANSWER THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE CONTRACT UNDER EVID. 452(H). THE CONTRACT DOES NOT CONCLUSIVELY SHOW THAT VIOLIN FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. WHETHER THE PARTIES SHARED A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP IS A FACTUAL ISSUE BASED ON THE PARTIES' CONDUCT. GCA ALSO READS PARAGRAPH 6 OF THE CONTRACT AS A CLAUSE THAT IMMUNIZES IT FROM LIABILITY. THAT READING VIOLATES CC 1668. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2013 Set For Trial On Feb-24-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dylan R. Hale, Esq. of Cooley LLP, Tel. 415.693.2140, for plaintiff/cross-defendant and moving party;
Goutam U. Jois, Esq. and Vanessa A. Pastora, Esq. of Gibson Dunn, Tel. 212.351.5363, for defendant/cross-complainant.

Judge Miller disclosed that she knows Gordon Atkinson. Their children went to school together and they served on the same non-profit board. These facts do not affect the Court's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The Court gave counsel the opportunity to ask questions. No questions were asked or objections were raised.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling as modifed. DEFENDANT GCA SAVVIAN ADVISORS, LLC's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled with 10 leave to answer The Court takes judicial notice of the contract under Evid. 452(h). The contract does not conclusively show that Violin fails to state a cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. Whether the parties shared a fiduciary relationship is a factual issue based on the parties' conduct. GCA also reads paragraph 6 of the contract as a clause that immunizes it from liability. That reading violates CC 1668. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
